title: dryas integrifolia subsp. chamissonis
intro: Dryas integrifolia subsp. chamissonis, also known as mountain avens, is a flowering plant that belongs to the rose family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating wounds, and is known to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The plant's leaves, roots, and flowers have been used in herbal preparations to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of various health conditions. In herbal preparations, mountain avens is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to take advantage of its health benefits, which include reducing fever, fighting infections, and soothing skin irritations.
